title: pellaea calomelanos var. swynnertoniana
intro: Pellaea calomelanos var. swynnertoniana, also known as Swynnerton's fern, is a variety of the P. calomelanos species of ferns native to Mozambique. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Pellaea calomelanos var. swynnertoniana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially treating conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
